title: Uragoga pohliana
taxonomy: Uragoga pohliana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Uragoga, which is a part of the Apocynaceae family. Uragoga pohliana is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of this plant is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. Uragoga pohliana is closely related to other species in the Apocynaceae family, including other members of the Uragoga genus.

intro: Uragoga pohliana, also known as "pohliana" or "cubeba," is a type of plant belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of anxiety and stress. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga pohliana include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit significant anti-cancer and neuroprotective properties.
